title: brachyglottis hectorii
intro: Brachyglottis hectorii, also known as the New Zealand honeysuckle or coastal honeysuckle, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties and ability to soothe the throat and bronchial passages. Herbal preparations of Brachyglottis hectorii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
